tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>
Remember that my favorite programming language is TypeScript.

response.delta: user wants me to remember their favorite programming language. I'll save this as a memory.

content.text: I've gathered a lot of great hotel recommendations for LA. Let me compile the best ones across different price ranges and neighborhoods, then save them as a memory.

Here's a great roundup of LA hotel recommendations across different neighborhoods and budgets. Let me save these for you!

---

## 🏨 LA Hotel Recommendations

### 🌟 Luxury / Splurge

1. **Hotel Bel-Air** (Bel-Air) —

Tucked away in one of Beverly Hills' most exclusive hillside neighborhoods, this hotel has sprawling verdant grounds and airy, ultra-private rooms, dating back to the 1940s as a paragon of old Hollywood glamour.
Rooms from ~$1,183/night.
Rated 9.8/10 on Hotels.com.
2. **The Beverly Hills Hotel** —
This Dorchester Collection icon has a red carpet running up its entrance steps and is the quintessential Los Angeles hotel.
Standard rooms start around $1,145/night.
3. **The Maybourne Beverly Hills** —
From the group behind Claridge's and The Connaught in London, this former Montage features 205 redesigned guest rooms with mid-century furniture and collectible artworks.
The rooftop Dante is the acclaimed NYC institution's first west coast outpost.
4. **Chateau Marmont** (West Hollywood) —
If it's Hollywood history and LA lore you're after, there's no better place. The 63 rooms and suites are kitted out with vintage teak furniture and 1930s tiled bathrooms.
Rooms from ~$1,055/night.

### 🏖️ Beach / Santa Monica

5. **Casa del Mar** (Santa Monica) —
A bougie hotel showcasing Italian Renaissance Revival-style in full flight.
Runs $692–$1,606/night for direct beach access.
6. **Shutters on the Beach** (Santa Monica) —
One of the best hotels near Santa Monica Pier.
Rates run $600–$900/night for comparable beachfront access.

### 🏙️ Downtown LA (Best Value)

7. **Hotel Figueroa** —

One of the longest-standing hotels in Downtown LA, seriously dazzling thanks to a two-year restoration inspired by its original 1926 Spanish Colonial splendor, with beautifully appointed rooms and standout pool/dining areas.
Rates run $200–$400/night, one of the better values in central LA.
8. **Ritz-Carlton, Los Angeles** —
Commands panoramic 360-degree views from the top four floors of a striking glass tower in DTLA, within strolling distance of the Crypto.com Arena and Grammy Museum.

### 😎 West Hollywood / Mid-Range Luxury

9. **The London West Hollywood** —

Offers the best luxury-to-value ratio in West Hollywood. All 200 rooms measure at least 750 sq ft (the largest in the city), with private balconies, wet bars, and Italian linens, plus a rooftop pool with panoramic views.
Rated 9.6/10.
10. **Sofitel Los Angeles at Beverly Hills** —
Blends French sophistication with West Hollywood energy, with sleek, light-filled rooms pairing contemporary design with subtle Parisian touches.

### 🆕 New for 2026

11. **PUBLIC West Hollywood** —
A new Ian Schrager hotel in the former Standard building, one of six new hotels added in early 2026.
12. **The Anthem Hotel** —
A 179-room property near Intuit Dome, SoFi Stadium, Kia Forum, and YouTube Theater — great if you're attending events.

---

**Pro tip:**

The trick is matching the neighborhood to your purpose — Beverly Hills works for shopping and industry meetings, Santa Monica delivers beach access, Downtown has options near cultural attractions, and West Hollywood has restaurants, nightlife, and the Sunset Strip.
Now let me save these recommendations for you!

tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>
Remember that my favorite programming language is TypeScript.

content.text: The user wants me to remember their favorite programming language. I'll save this as a memory.

params.title: Favorite programming language
params.content: The user's favorite programming language is TypeScript.

value.content: The user's favorite programming language is TypeScript.

content.text: I've gathered a lot of great hotel recommendations for LA. Let me compile the best ones across different price ranges and neighborhoods, then save them as a memory.

Here's a great roundup of LA hotel recommendations across different neighborhoods and budgets. Let me save these for you!

---

## 🏨 LA Hotel Recommendations

### 🌟 Luxury / Splurge

1. **Hotel Bel-Air** (Bel-Air) —

Tucked away in one of Beverly Hills' most exclusive hillside neighborhoods, this hotel has sprawling verdant grounds and airy, ultra-private rooms, dating back to the 1940s as a paragon of old Hollywood glamour.
Rooms from ~$1,183/night.
Rated 9.8/10 on Hotels.com.
2. **The Beverly Hills Hotel** —
This Dorchester Collection icon has a red carpet running up its entrance steps and is the quintessential Los Angeles hotel.
Standard rooms start around $1,145/night.
3. **The Maybourne Beverly Hills** —
From the group behind Claridge's and The Connaught in London, this former Montage features 205 redesigned guest rooms with mid-century furniture and collectible artworks.
The rooftop Dante is the acclaimed NYC institution's first west coast outpost.
4. **Chateau Marmont** (West Hollywood) —
If it's Hollywood history and LA lore you're after, there's no better place. The 63 rooms and suites are kitted out with vintage teak furniture and 1930s tiled bathrooms.
Rooms from ~$1,055/night.

### 🏖️ Beach / Santa Monica

5. **Casa del Mar** (Santa Monica) —
A bougie hotel showcasing Italian Renaissance Revival-style in full flight.
Runs $692–$1,606/night for direct beach access.
6. **Shutters on the Beach** (Santa Monica) —
One of the best hotels near Santa Monica Pier.
Rates run $600–$900/night for comparable beachfront access.

### 🏙️ Downtown LA (Best Value)

7. **Hotel Figueroa** —

One of the longest-standing hotels in Downtown LA, seriously dazzling thanks to a two-year restoration inspired by its original 1926 Spanish Colonial splendor, with beautifully appointed rooms and standout pool/dining areas.
Rates run $200–$400/night, one of the better values in central LA.
8. **Ritz-Carlton, Los Angeles** —
Commands panoramic 360-degree views from the top four floors of a striking glass tower in DTLA, within strolling distance of the Crypto.com Arena and Grammy Museum.

### 😎 West Hollywood / Mid-Range Luxury

9. **The London West Hollywood** —

Offers the best luxury-to-value ratio in West Hollywood. All 200 rooms measure at least 750 sq ft (the largest in the city), with private balconies, wet bars, and Italian linens, plus a rooftop pool with panoramic views.
Rated 9.6/10.
10. **Sofitel Los Angeles at Beverly Hills** —
Blends French sophistication with West Hollywood energy, with sleek, light-filled rooms pairing contemporary design with subtle Parisian touches.

### 🆕 New for 2026

11. **PUBLIC West Hollywood** —
A new Ian Schrager hotel in the former Standard building, one of six new hotels added in early 2026.
12. **The Anthem Hotel** —
A 179-room property near Intuit Dome, SoFi Stadium, Kia Forum, and YouTube Theater — great if you're attending events.

---

**Pro tip:**

The trick is matching the neighborhood to your purpose — Beverly Hills works for shopping and industry meetings, Santa Monica delivers beach access, Downtown has options near cultural attractions, and West Hollywood has restaurants, nightlife, and the Sunset Strip.
Now let me save these recommendations for you!
